notes: all combat numbers are simulator output on unverified constants
checkpoint levels are provisional (no level timeline in the match feed)
evidence deltas carry finished-inventory survivorship bias
support model counts one beneficiary and active-ability heals/shields only (passive heals, CC, and damage-reduction utility are not scored)
the sim build reproduces only 33% of the field's core for Riktor (it never builds Xenia, Frosted Lure) — some of those items ride mechanics the sim doesn't model yet

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Zinx's main threat is Ricochet; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.
They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Dekker deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.
You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Phase's main threat is Energy Lance; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Narbash deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. Khaimera's main threat is Cull; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Cursed Scroll: their 3s all-in loses 18% of its bite, at the cost of 15% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Serath's main threat is Chastise; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Cursed Scroll: their 3s all-in loses 18% of its bite, at the cost of 16% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Boris deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Cursed Scroll: their 3s all-in loses 18% of its bite, at the cost of 16% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Kwang ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Cursed Scroll: their 3s all-in loses 14% of its bite, at the cost of 16% of this kit's damage.
